title: Counting Sides and Vertices of a Rectangle

steps.work: A side is a straight line that forms part of the shape's boundary.
steps.explanation: Each straight edge of a shape is one side.

steps.instruction: Count the sides of a rectangle.
steps.work: Top (1), right (2), bottom (3), left (4). A rectangle has 4 sides.
steps.explanation: Go around the shape and count each straight edge.

steps.work: A vertex is a point where two sides meet. The plural of vertex is vertices.
steps.explanation: Each corner where two sides connect is one vertex.

steps.instruction: Count the vertices of the rectangle.
steps.work: Top-left (1), top-right (2), bottom-right (3), bottom-left (4). A rectangle has 4 vertices.
steps.explanation: The rectangle has 4 corners, so it has 4 vertices — the same as the number of sides.

steps.instruction: Count sides and vertices of a triangle.
Count sides and vertices of a square.
Count sides and vertices of a pentagon.

steps.work: The number of sides always equals the number of vertices: 3 = 3, 4 = 4, 5 = 5.
steps.explanation: For any polygon (flat shape with straight sides), the number of sides is always equal to the number of vertices. This is because each side connects two vertices, and each vertex connects two sides.
